description: arXiv Category Taxonomy - Complete list of subject classifications

cs.AI.name: Artificial Intelligence
cs.AI.description: Covers all areas of AI except Vision, Robotics, Machine Learning, Multiagent Systems, and Computation and Language (Natural Language Processing), which have separate subject areas. In particular, includes Expert Systems, Theorem Proving, Knowledge Representation, Planning, and Uncertainty in AI.

cs.AR.name: Hardware Architecture
cs.AR.description: Covers systems organization and hardware architecture.

cs.CC.name: Computational Complexity
cs.CC.description: Covers models of computation, complexity classes, structural complexity, complexity tradeoffs, upper and lower bounds.

cs.CE.name: Computational Engineering, Finance, and Science
cs.CE.full_name: Computer Science - Computational Engineering, Finance, and Science
cs.CE.description: Covers applications of computer science to the mathematical modeling of complex systems in the fields of science, engineering, and finance.

cs.CG.name: Computational Geometry
cs.CG.description: Covers computational geometry algorithms and techniques.

cs.CL.name: Computation and Language
cs.CL.description: Covers natural language processing. Includes work on computational linguistics, speech, text retrieval, etc.

cs.CR.name: Cryptography and Security
cs.CR.description: Covers all areas of cryptography and security including authentication, public key cryptosystems, proof-carrying code, etc.

cs.CV.name: Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
cs.CV.full_name: Computer Science - Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
cs.CV.description: Covers image processing, computer vision, pattern recognition, and scene understanding.

cs.CY.name: Computers and Society
cs.CY.description: Covers impact of computers on society, computer ethics, information technology and public policy, legal aspects of computing, computers and education.

cs.DB.name: Databases
cs.DB.description: Covers database management, datamining, and data processing.

cs.DC.name: Distributed, Parallel, and Cluster Computing
cs.DC.full_name: Computer Science - Distributed, Parallel, and Cluster Computing
cs.DC.description: Covers fault-tolerance, distributed algorithms, stability, parallel computation, and cluster computing.

cs.DL.name: Digital Libraries
cs.DL.description: Covers all aspects of the digital library design and document and text creation.

cs.DM.name: Discrete Mathematics
cs.DM.description: Covers combinatorics, graph theory, applications of probability.

cs.DS.name: Data Structures and Algorithms
cs.DS.full_name: Computer Science - Data Structures and Algorithms
cs.DS.description: Covers data structures and analysis of algorithms.

cs.ET.name: Emerging Technologies
cs.ET.description: Covers approaches to information processing based on alternatives to silicon CMOS-based technologies.

cs.FL.name: Formal Languages and Automata Theory
cs.FL.full_name: Computer Science - Formal Languages and Automata Theory
cs.FL.description: Covers automata theory, formal language theory, grammars, and combinatorics on words.

cs.GL.name: General Literature
cs.GL.description: Covers introductory material, survey material, predictions of future trends, biographies, and miscellaneous computer-science related material.

cs.GR.name: Graphics
cs.GR.description: Covers all aspects of computer graphics.

cs.GT.name: Computer Science and Game Theory
cs.GT.full_name: Computer Science - Computer Science and Game Theory
cs.GT.description: Covers all theoretical and applied aspects at the intersection of computer science and game theory.

cs.HC.name: Human-Computer Interaction
cs.HC.description: Covers human factors, user interfaces, and collaborative computing.

cs.IR.name: Information Retrieval
cs.IR.description: Covers indexing, dictionaries, retrieval, content and analysis.

cs.IT.name: Information Theory
cs.IT.description: Covers theoretical and experimental aspects of information theory and coding.

cs.LG.name: Machine Learning
cs.LG.description: Papers on all aspects of machine learning research (supervised, unsupervised, reinforcement learning, bandit problems, and so on) including also robustness, explanation, fairness, and methodology.

cs.LO.name: Logic in Computer Science
cs.LO.full_name: Computer Science - Logic in Computer Science
cs.LO.description: Covers all aspects of logic in computer science, including finite model theory, logics of programs, modal logic, and program verification.

cs.MA.name: Multiagent Systems
cs.MA.description: Covers multiagent systems, distributed artificial intelligence, intelligent agents, coordinated interactions, and practical applications.

cs.MM.name: Multimedia
cs.MM.description: Covers multimedia computing, including audio, video, and multimodal processing.

cs.MS.name: Mathematical Software
cs.MS.description: Covers mathematical software development and applications.

cs.NA.name: Numerical Analysis
cs.NA.description: Covers numerical algorithms for problems in analysis and algebra, scientific computation.

cs.NE.name: Neural and Evolutionary Computing
cs.NE.full_name: Computer Science - Neural and Evolutionary Computing
cs.NE.description: Covers neural networks, connectionism, genetic algorithms, artificial life, adaptive behavior.

cs.NI.name: Networking and Internet Architecture
cs.NI.full_name: Computer Science - Networking and Internet Architecture
cs.NI.description: Covers all aspects of computer communication networks, including network architecture and design, network protocols, and internetwork standards.

cs.OH.name: Other Computer Science
cs.OH.description: This is the classification to use for documents that do not fit anywhere else.

cs.OS.name: Operating Systems
cs.OS.description: Covers operating systems design and implementation.

cs.PF.name: Performance
cs.PF.description: Covers performance measurement and evaluation, queueing, and simulation.

cs.PL.name: Programming Languages
cs.PL.description: Covers programming language semantics, language features, programming approaches.

cs.RO.name: Robotics
cs.RO.description: Covers robotics research including robot design, control, planning, and applications.

cs.SC.name: Symbolic Computation
cs.SC.description: Covers symbolic computation and computer algebra.

cs.SD.name: Sound
cs.SD.description: Covers all aspects of computing with sound, and sound as an information channel.

cs.SE.name: Software Engineering
cs.SE.description: Covers design tools, software metrics, testing and debugging, programming environments, etc.

cs.SI.name: Social and Information Networks
cs.SI.full_name: Computer Science - Social and Information Networks
cs.SI.description: Covers the design, analysis, and modeling of social and information networks.

cs.SY.name: Systems and Control
cs.SY.description: Covers theoretical and experimental research covering all facets of automatic control systems.

eess.AS.name: Audio and Speech Processing
eess.AS.full_name: Electrical Engineering and Systems Science - Audio and Speech Processing
eess.AS.description: Theory and methods for processing signals representing audio, speech, and language, and their applications.

eess.IV.name: Image and Video Processing
eess.IV.full_name: Electrical Engineering and Systems Science - Image and Video Processing
eess.IV.description: Theory, algorithms, and architectures for the formation, capture, processing, communication, analysis, and display of images, video, and multidimensional signals.

eess.SP.name: Signal Processing
eess.SP.full_name: Electrical Engineering and Systems Science - Signal Processing
eess.SP.description: Theory, algorithms, performance analysis and applications of signal and data analysis.

eess.SY.name: Systems and Control
eess.SY.full_name: Electrical Engineering and Systems Science - Systems and Control
eess.SY.description: Theoretical and experimental research covering all facets of automatic control systems.

stat.AP.name: Applications
stat.AP.description: Applications of statistical methods to various domains.

stat.ME.name: Methodology
stat.ME.description: Statistical methodology including design, surveys, model selection, etc.

stat.ML.name: Machine Learning
stat.ML.description: Covers machine learning papers with a statistical or theoretical grounding.

stat.OT.name: Other Statistics
stat.OT.description: Work in statistics that does not fit into the other stat classifications.

stat.TH.name: Statistics Theory
stat.TH.description: Asymptotics, Bayesian Inference, Decision Theory, Estimation, Foundations, Inference, Testing.

math.AC.name: Commutative Algebra
math.AC.description: Commutative rings, modules, ideals, homological algebra, computational aspects.

math.AG.name: Algebraic Geometry
math.AG.description: Algebraic varieties, stacks, sheaves, schemes, moduli spaces.

math.AP.name: Analysis of PDEs
math.AP.description: Existence and uniqueness, boundary conditions, linear and non-linear operators.

math.AT.name: Algebraic Topology
math.AT.description: Homotopy theory, homological algebra, algebraic treatments of manifolds.

math.CA.name: Classical Analysis and ODEs
math.CA.description: Special functions, orthogonal polynomials, harmonic analysis, ODEs.

math.CO.name: Combinatorics
math.CO.description: Discrete mathematics, graph theory, enumeration, combinatorial optimization.

math.DG.name: Differential Geometry
math.DG.description: Complex, contact, Riemannian, pseudo-Riemannian and Finsler geometry.

math.DS.name: Dynamical Systems
math.DS.description: Dynamics of differential equations and flows, mechanics, iterations.

math.FA.name: Functional Analysis
math.FA.description: Banach spaces, function spaces, real functions, integral transforms.

math.GM.name: General Mathematics
math.GM.description: Mathematical material of general interest, topics not covered elsewhere.

math.GR.name: Group Theory
math.GR.description: Finite groups, topological groups, representation theory, cohomology.

math.GT.name: Geometric Topology
math.GT.description: Manifolds, orbifolds, polyhedra, cell complexes, foliations.

math.IT.name: Information Theory
math.IT.description: Covers theoretical and experimental aspects of information theory and coding.

math.LO.name: Logic
math.LO.description: Logic, set theory, point-set topology, formal mathematics.

math.NA.name: Numerical Analysis
math.NA.description: Numerical algorithms for problems in analysis and algebra.

math.NT.name: Number Theory
math.NT.description: Prime numbers, diophantine equations, analytic number theory.

math.OC.name: Optimization and Control
math.OC.description: Operations research, linear programming, control theory, systems theory.

math.PR.name: Probability
math.PR.description: Theory and applications of probability and stochastic processes.

math.QA.name: Quantum Algebra
math.QA.description: Quantum groups, skein theories, operadic and diagrammatic algebra.

math.RA.name: Rings and Algebras
math.RA.description: Non-commutative rings and algebras, non-associative algebras.

math.RT.name: Representation Theory
math.RT.description: Linear representations of algebras and groups, Lie theory.

math.SP.name: Spectral Theory
math.SP.description: Schrödinger operators, operators on manifolds, general differential operators.

math.CT.name: Category Theory
math.CT.description: Enriched categories, topoi, abelian categories, monoidal categories, homological algebra.

math.CV.name: Complex Variables
math.CV.description: Holomorphic functions, automorphic group actions and forms, pseudoconvexity, complex geometry.

math.GN.name: General Topology
math.GN.description: Continuum theory, point-set topology, spaces with algebraic structure, foundations, dimension theory.

math.HO.name: History and Overview
math.HO.description: Biographies, philosophy of mathematics, mathematics education, recreational mathematics.

math.KT.name: K-Theory and Homology
math.KT.description: Algebraic and topological K-theory, relations with topology, commutative algebra, and operator algebras.

math.MG.name: Metric Geometry
math.MG.description: Euclidean, hyperbolic, discrete, convex, coarse geometry, comparisons in Riemannian geometry.

math.MP.name: Mathematical Physics
math.MP.description: Application of mathematics to problems in physics, mathematically rigorous formulations of physical theories.

math.OA.name: Operator Algebras
math.OA.description: Algebras of operators on Hilbert space, C*-algebras, von Neumann algebras, non-commutative geometry.

math.SG.name: Symplectic Geometry
math.SG.description: Hamiltonian systems, symplectic flows, classical integrable systems.

physics.acc-ph.name: Accelerator Physics
physics.acc-ph.description: Accelerator theory and simulation, accelerator technology and experiments.

physics.ao-ph.name: Atmospheric and Oceanic Physics
physics.ao-ph.description: Atmospheric and oceanic physics and physical chemistry, biogeophysics, and climate science.

physics.app-ph.name: Applied Physics
physics.app-ph.description: Applications of physics to new technology.

physics.atm-clus.name: Atomic and Molecular Clusters
physics.atm-clus.description: Atomic and molecular clusters, nanoparticles: geometric, electronic, optical, chemical, magnetic properties.

physics.atom-ph.name: Atomic Physics
physics.atom-ph.description: Atomic and molecular structure, spectra, collisions, and data.

physics.bio-ph.name: Biological Physics
physics.bio-ph.description: Molecular biophysics, cellular biophysics, neurological biophysics.

physics.chem-ph.name: Chemical Physics
physics.chem-ph.description: Experimental, computational, and theoretical physics of atoms, molecules, and clusters.

physics.class-ph.name: Classical Physics
physics.class-ph.description: Newtonian and relativistic dynamics, Maxwell's equations, classical waves.

physics.comp-ph.name: Computational Physics
physics.comp-ph.description: All aspects of computational science applied to physics.

physics.data-an.name: Data Analysis, Statistics and Probability
physics.data-an.full_name: Physics - Data Analysis, Statistics and Probability
physics.data-an.description: Methods, software and hardware for physics data analysis.

physics.ed-ph.name: Physics Education
physics.ed-ph.description: Research on teaching and learning in physics, misconceptions, assessment.

physics.hist-ph.name: History and Philosophy of Physics
physics.hist-ph.full_name: Physics - History and Philosophy of Physics
physics.hist-ph.description: History and philosophy of all branches of physics, astrophysics, and cosmology.

physics.ins-det.name: Instrumentation and Detectors
physics.ins-det.description: Instrumentation and detectors for research in natural science.

physics.med-ph.name: Medical Physics
physics.med-ph.description: Radiation therapy, radiation dosimetry, biomedical imaging.

physics.optics.name: Optics
physics.optics.description: Adaptive optics, fiber optics, lasers, optical devices.

physics.plasm-ph.name: Plasma Physics
physics.plasm-ph.description: Fundamental plasma physics, magnetically confined plasmas.

physics.pop-ph.name: Popular Physics
physics.pop-ph.description: Popular physics, physics for general audience.

physics.soc-ph.name: Physics and Society
physics.soc-ph.description: Structure, dynamics and collective behavior of societies and groups, quantitative analysis of social networks.

physics.space-ph.name: Space Physics
physics.space-ph.description: Space plasma physics, heliophysics, space weather, planetary magnetospheres.

quant-ph.name: Quantum Physics
quant-ph.description: Quantum mechanics, quantum information, quantum computing.

q-bio.BM.name: Biomolecules
q-bio.BM.description: DNA, RNA, proteins, lipids, etc.; molecular structures and folding kinetics.

q-bio.GN.name: Genomics
q-bio.GN.description: DNA sequencing and assembly, gene finding, genomic structure.

q-bio.MN.name: Molecular Networks
q-bio.MN.description: Gene regulation, signal transduction, proteomics, metabolomics.

q-bio.NC.name: Neurons and Cognition
q-bio.NC.description: Synapse, cortex, neuronal dynamics, neural network, sensorimotor control.

q-bio.QM.name: Quantitative Methods
q-bio.QM.description: Experimental, numerical, statistical and mathematical contributions to biology.

q-bio.SC.name: Subcellular Processes
q-bio.SC.description: Assembly and control of subcellular structures, molecular motors, transport, mitosis and meiosis.

q-bio.OT.name: Other Quantitative Biology
q-bio.OT.description: Work in quantitative biology that does not fit into the other q-bio classifications.

econ.GN.name: General Economics
econ.GN.description: General methodological, applied, and empirical contributions to economics.

econ.TH.name: Theoretical Economics
econ.TH.description: Contract Theory, Decision Theory, Game Theory, General Equilibrium.

q-fin.CP.name: Computational Finance
q-fin.CP.description: Computational methods with applications to financial modeling.

q-fin.EC.name: Economics
q-fin.EC.description: Economics, including micro and macro economics, international economics, theory of the firm, labor economics.

q-fin.GN.name: General Finance
q-fin.GN.description: Development of general quantitative methodologies with applications in finance.

q-fin.MF.name: Mathematical Finance
q-fin.MF.description: Mathematical and analytical methods of finance.

q-fin.PM.name: Portfolio Management
q-fin.PM.description: Security selection, capital allocation, investment strategies.

q-fin.PR.name: Pricing of Securities
q-fin.PR.description: Valuation and hedging of financial securities and derivatives.

q-fin.RM.name: Risk Management
q-fin.RM.description: Measurement and management of financial risks.

q-fin.ST.name: Statistical Finance
q-fin.ST.description: Statistical and econophysics analyses with applications to financial markets.

q-fin.TR.name: Trading and Market Microstructure
q-fin.TR.full_name: Quantitative Finance - Trading and Market Microstructure

astro-ph.CO.name: Cosmology and Nongalactic Astrophysics
astro-ph.CO.description: Phenomenology of early universe, cosmic microwave background, cosmological parameters.

astro-ph.EP.name: Earth and Planetary Astrophysics
astro-ph.EP.description: Planetary physics, extrasolar planets, solar system.

astro-ph.GA.name: Astrophysics of Galaxies
astro-ph.GA.description: Galaxies, star clusters, interstellar medium, galactic structure.

astro-ph.HE.name: High Energy Astrophysical Phenomena
astro-ph.HE.description: Cosmic rays, gamma ray astronomy, supernovae, black holes.

astro-ph.IM.name: Instrumentation and Methods for Astrophysics
astro-ph.IM.full_name: Astrophysics - Instrumentation and Methods for Astrophysics
astro-ph.IM.description: Detector and telescope design, data analysis methods.

astro-ph.SR.name: Solar and Stellar Astrophysics
astro-ph.SR.description: Stars, stellar evolution, binary systems, stellar atmospheres.

cond-mat.dis-nn.name: Disordered Systems and Neural Networks
cond-mat.dis-nn.full_name: Condensed Matter - Disordered Systems and Neural Networks
cond-mat.dis-nn.description: Glasses, spin glasses, random systems, neural networks.

cond-mat.mes-hall.name: Mesoscale and Nanoscale Physics
cond-mat.mes-hall.full_name: Condensed Matter - Mesoscale and Nanoscale Physics
cond-mat.mes-hall.description: Quantum dots, nanotubes, graphene, 2D electron gases.

cond-mat.mtrl-sci.name: Materials Science
cond-mat.mtrl-sci.description: Techniques, synthesis, characterization, structural phase transitions.

cond-mat.quant-gas.name: Quantum Gases
cond-mat.quant-gas.description: Ultracold atomic gases, Bose-Einstein condensation, optical lattices.

cond-mat.soft.name: Soft Condensed Matter
cond-mat.soft.description: Membranes, polymers, liquid crystals, colloids, granular matter.

cond-mat.stat-mech.name: Statistical Mechanics
cond-mat.stat-mech.description: Phase transitions, thermodynamics, field theory, renormalization group.

cond-mat.str-el.name: Strongly Correlated Electrons
cond-mat.str-el.description: Quantum magnetism, non-Fermi liquids, metal-insulator transitions.

cond-mat.other.name: Other Condensed Matter
cond-mat.other.description: Work in condensed matter that does not fit into the other cond-mat classifications.

nlin.AO.name: Adaptation and Self-Organizing Systems
nlin.AO.full_name: Nonlinear Sciences - Adaptation and Self-Organizing Systems
nlin.AO.description: Adaptation, self-organizing systems, statistical physics, fluctuating systems, stochastic processes.

nlin.CD.name: Chaotic Dynamics
nlin.CD.description: Dynamical systems, chaos, quantum chaos, topological dynamics, cycle expansions, turbulence.

nlin.CG.name: Cellular Automata and Lattice Gases
nlin.CG.full_name: Nonlinear Sciences - Cellular Automata and Lattice Gases
nlin.CG.description: Computational methods, time series analysis, signal processing, wavelets, lattice gases.

nlin.PS.name: Pattern Formation and Solitons
nlin.PS.full_name: Nonlinear Sciences - Pattern Formation and Solitons

nlin.SI.name: Exactly Solvable and Integrable Systems
nlin.SI.full_name: Nonlinear Sciences - Exactly Solvable and Integrable Systems
nlin.SI.description: Exactly solvable systems, integrable PDEs, integrable ODEs, Painleve analysis.

gr-qc.name: General Relativity and Quantum Cosmology
gr-qc.description: General relativity, quantum cosmology, gravitational waves, alternative theories of gravity, quantum gravity.

hep-ex.name: High Energy Physics - Experiment
hep-ex.description: Results from high-energy/particle physics experiments and prospects for future experimental results.

hep-lat.name: High Energy Physics - Lattice
hep-lat.description: Lattice field theory, phenomenology from lattice field theory, algorithms for lattice field theory.

hep-ph.name: High Energy Physics - Phenomenology
hep-ph.description: Theoretical particle physics and its interrelation with experiment.

hep-th.name: High Energy Physics - Theory
hep-th.description: Formal aspects of quantum field theory, string theory, supersymmetry and supergravity.

math-ph.name: Mathematical Physics
math-ph.description: Application of mathematics to problems in physics, mathematically rigorous formulations of physical theories.

nucl-ex.name: Nuclear Experiment
nucl-ex.description: Results from experimental nuclear physics including fundamental interactions and relativistic heavy-ion collisions.

nucl-th.name: Nuclear Theory
nucl-th.description: Theory of nuclear structure, nuclear equation of states, theory of nuclear reactions.
